title: Clear Pet scan and MRI
body:
So, in October I was diagnosed with stage 4b colorectal cancer. I ended up having to get a colostomy bag, and during surgery they removed a 17cm Tumor and an 8cm tumor. However I still had multiple areas of my rectum that were cancerous.. I started chemo- FOLFOX In November of last year. 

(Bevacizumab, Oxaliplatin,Leucovorin, Fluorouracil)

My oncologist ordered the routine Petscan 2 weeks ago, and I was going to have my 6th infusion (final of round 1) this past Monday. However, my petscan showed ALL CLEAR! She the ordered an MRI to double check, and see about the possibility of the colostomy reversal... MRI was also CLEAR! We're still waiting on the Signatara results..When I started this everyone basically told me I had a 10% chance of beating this, and to expect at least a year of chemo... to which I replied "hold my beer"... I've felt cancer free pretty much since infusion #2- my oncology team continues to tell me not to get my hopes up... NOW my question is this: What are the odds of it coming back!?... And could the signatara still show something? I've already requested to have a month or so off chemo , and I have to anyway since my reversal

Is scheduled for mar 30... but I genuinely don't know if I can do any more infusions. I'm a generally happy high energy person, and chemo has turned me into the most depressed, angry wife and mom ever. Am I getting too excited too soon? Or are there any alternatives you've tried aside from chemo (and yes I've been taking ivermectin etc outside of chemo).... Just trying to mentally prepare for whatever hits may come next..
